growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Rich medium (mGAM / WCA / Schaedler) with and without supplemental mucin (Sigma M1778, type III); positive control

up.method: known mucin forager; better growth with mucin (positive control)

growth_records.cite: Ducarmon et al. Cayman enables large-scale analysis of gut microbiome carbohydrate-active enzyme repertoires. Nat Microbiol (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41564-026-02318-2. [PMC13236585]
growth_records.snippet: We observed better growth (faster outgrowth, increased growth rates and/or higher yields) in the presence of mucin for all three species and the positive control, Akkermansia muciniphila, in Wilkins-Chalgren Anaerobe (WCA) medium
growth_records.notes: Positive-control mucin forager. OD578 growth curves only (Extended Data Fig.3); no numeric growth rate/flux reported. Better growth with mucin (qualitative). Commercial undefined media; composition not stated.

growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Akkermansia muciniphila YL44 (Verrucomicrobia), Max OD 0.32, Mean μ ± SD 0.09 ± 0.01. 'grew only to a low maximum OD and did not grow in the absence of mucin...suggesting that mucin is its main carbon source'.
growth_records.notes: μ=0.09 h^-1 (±0.01) reported — slowest grower; mucin-dependent. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.09 = 7.702 h. Max OD600 = 0.32. Malate supplementation (10 mM) 'almost doubled' its growth rate (Fig 4A, exact value not in text). NOTE: Acutalibacter muris (KB18) and Turicimonas muris (YL45) did not grow in mBHI (dashes in Table 1) so no records emitted for them.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
